heading: Sixto timing-graph curves compress to a shared branch family with one positive-branch outlier
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto, timing-graph, compression, branch-family]
body: Built `sixto_graph_curve_compression_packet.json` and `build_sixto_graph_curve_compression_packet.py` to test whether the exact Sixto timing-graph interpolation packet compresses under anchor normalization. Using branch coordinates `left: t=x/crossover_x, y_norm=y/abs(dip_y)` and `right: t=(x-crossover_x)/(1212-crossover_x), y_norm=y/peak_y`, both left and right medoids are `curve_graph_green`. The left-branch template is shared by all four curves within the configured envelope (`mean_abs <= 0.14`, `max_abs <= 0.30`): light-green mean/max `0.123502 / 0.209836`, cyan `0.041245 / 0.151714`, blue `0.031285 / 0.170492`. The right-branch template is shared by light-green and blue but not cyan: light-green `0.068581 / 0.189274`, blue `0.031041 / 0.173477`, cyan `0.121343 / 0.699542`. Honest verdict: there is a real compressed branch grammar under the exact interpolation laws, but it is not a single fully shared positive branch; the cyan right branch remains the only clear outlier under this normalization.
